axis: Phase 3 chain bench + policy resolution via worker relocation to hexa-lang/stdlib

harness.interpreter: /Users/ghost/.hx/bin/hexa_real (bypass dispatcher; the default `hexa` shim auto-routed to remote ubu2 where numpy is unavailable, so all WAV writes silently failed and timings were meaningless ~46ms shell-only)
harness.ms_units: wallclock per call, captured via date +%s%N before/after the organ call inside hexa, divided by 1e6 in this JSON

vocal_only.note: Matches Phase 2 POC (which reported 2.34x for vocal). Single-organ workload bottoms out at the bash exec()-fork + FIFO IPC floor (~50-70ms).

breath_only.note: Largest single-organ win because baseline pays full scipy.signal.butter+sosfilt import per call (~1s on this Darwin host). Worker pre-imports butter+sosfilt at boot, so per-call cost collapses to the ~67ms IPC floor. The breath cold call (71.93ms) is essentially warm because worker stays alive across the test set; the first per-organ call only pays the ~961ms worker spawn that vocal absorbed.

vocal_lf_only.note: vocal_lf baseline is ~28ms slower than vocal due to Liljencrants-Fant np.where branching and peak-norm pass; worker squashes that to the same IPC floor as vocal because numpy is already loaded.

vocal_pipe_chain.design_prediction_60x_check: MISS. Phase 2 design projected 60x on chain (1177ms -> ~20ms). Achieved 8-10x (~150ms warm). Each chain link is two _run_py calls (vocal + pipe), so the chain pays 2x the bash exec() floor (~95-100ms) plus 2x scipy lfilter import savings on baseline. Worker eliminates per-call scipy import (~500ms saved per pipe call) but cannot eliminate the bash-fork + FIFO open/close (~70ms each, x2 links = ~140-150ms observed). To approach 60x, hexa runtime would need a persistent-pipe primitive that bypasses the per-call /bin/bash + /bin/cat + /bin/base64 fork chain. Filed as ω-runtime-1 follow-up.
vocal_pipe_chain.note_outliers: Baseline chain runs 4 and 5 (1830, 1901 ms) are ~600ms above the first 3 — likely transient system load (Darwin filesystem cache contention or background indexer). first3-mean is the more honest baseline; all5-mean inflates the speedup. Worker chain runs are tight (137-157ms, sigma ~8ms) so the bottleneck is genuinely deterministic IPC overhead, not scipy.

phase3_bench.per_organ_speedup_observations: Worker speedup is dominated by which scipy modules the baseline reimports each call: (a) breath does butter+sosfilt -> ~1000ms saved per call -> 16.6x; (b) chain does lfilter -> ~500ms saved per pipe link -> 8-10x; (c) vocal/vocal_lf do only numpy -> ~80ms saved -> 2.1-2.4x. The bash exec()+FIFO IPC floor is ~67-75ms regardless. So the design's 60x target is achievable only when shell+IPC overhead drops below 5ms (i.e. hexa runtime owns the FIFO write/read primitive directly).

policy_resolution.rationale: anima/.gitignore enforces R37/AN13/L3-PY 'no python source in anima/' rule. hexa-lang/.gitignore has no .py exclusion. Moving the worker source-of-truth to hexa-lang/stdlib/ keeps the rule intact while making the worker tracked (deployable on fresh checkout via `git add`) — the worker is conceptually a hexa-runtime helper anyway, not anima domain code.

files_updated.change: Replaced fixed `WORKER_PY=$(dirname $0)/anima_audio_worker.py` with 3-tier resolution: (1) explicit $ANIMA_AUDIO_WORKER_PY env if set+exists, (2) $HEXA_LANG/stdlib/anima_audio_worker.py if env set, (3) absolute fallback /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/stdlib/anima_audio_worker.py. Emits NDJSON error and exits 1 if none found.

files_unchanged: /Users/ghost/core/anima/anima-speak/anima_audio.hexa (WORKER_CALL_SH points to the .sh which stays in anima-speak; only the .py reference inside the .sh changed)

verification_after_relocation.evidence: Phase 3 bench (above) ran ANIMA_AUDIO_WORKER=1 against the relocated worker and produced 25 WAVs (5 vocal + 5 breath + 5 vocal_lf + 5 chain*[vocal,pipe]) all exactly 4844 bytes (100ms @ 24kHz mono int16 + 44-byte WAV header). Worker spawned via the new resolution path (no /Users/ghost/core/anima/anima-speak/anima_audio_worker.py existed at run time — verified by `ls` returning No such file), confirming the .sh found the relocated copy.

git_status_after.anima_repo: anima-speak/_audio_worker_call.sh modified; anima-speak/anima_audio_worker.py absent (gitignore matches it via **/*.py rule which now has nothing to ignore)
git_status_after.hexa_lang_repo: stdlib/anima_audio_worker.py untracked, NOT gitignored (verified with `git check-ignore -v` returning empty)
git_status_after.anima_ignore_check: .gitignore:3:**/*.py would still match the path if it existed in anima — rule intact

phase3_bug_findings.severity: blocker (workaround applied, not in production code)
phase3_bug_findings.title: default `hexa` shim auto-routes to remote ubu2 which lacks numpy
phase3_bug_findings.evidence: First baseline bench using /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/hexa printed `hexa_remote: ubu2 에서 원격 실행 중` then `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'numpy'` for every organ; timings dropped to ~46ms (just shell+exec wallclock, no actual python work). 0 WAVs were produced.
phase3_bug_findings.resolution: Used /Users/ghost/.hx/bin/hexa_real for both baseline and worker benches to force local execution. This is a harness concern, not a regression in anima_audio.hexa.
phase3_bug_findings.follow_up: Either (a) install numpy/scipy on ubu2/ubu1 remotes, or (b) add a local-only annotation to anima_audio.hexa, or (c) document that ANIMA_AUDIO_* benches must run against hexa_real.

phase3_bug_findings.title: shell pipeline `| head -10` truncates hexa output and triggers SIGPIPE deadlock
phase3_bug_findings.evidence: First worker-bench attempt was piped through `grep ... | head -10` which closed stdin on the upstream after 10 lines, leaving hexa_real waiting for the worker IPC and the worker waiting for parent_alive — produced exit 144 after kill. Replaced with `> /tmp/bench_worker.out` redirect. No code change needed.

phase3_bug_findings.title: no per-organ allowlist needed
phase3_bug_findings.evidence: All 4 tested organs (vocal, breath, pipe-via-chain, vocal_lf) work identically through the worker. No namespace contamination, no scipy-fn missing, no FIFO deadlock. Worker pre-imports cover all 4 organs' scipy needs (lfilter, lfiltic, butter, sosfilt, fftconvolve, resample_poly). pipe inside the chain validates lfilter dispatch.

deferred_to_later_phases: Phase 3 remaining organs — pipe3, pipe_glide, tone, chirp, silence, lpf/hpf/bpf/notch, gain, envelope, reverb, cat, mix (all use _run_py; based on this phase's evidence, no per-organ work needed beyond a smoke check; worker handles them via the same dispatch path)

ω-runtime-1 hexa persistent-pipe primitive — direct FIFO write/read from hexa runtime bypassing /bin/bash + /bin/cat + /bin/base64 fork chain, projected to drop chain warm from 148ms to <20ms and approach the design's 60x target
harness portability — replace /Users/ghost/.hx/bin/hexa_real explicit path with a `hexa --local` flag or `HEXA_FORCE_LOCAL=1` env once the dispatcher exposes it
cleanup_evidence: post-bench: pgrep anima_audio_worker -> 0; pgrep hexa_real -> 0; ls /tmp/anima_audio_worker_* -> no matches; ls /tmp/anima_audio_*.wav -> no matches; rm /Users/ghost/core/anima/anima-speak/_omega_audio_2_phase3_bench.hexa (harness removed); /tmp/bench_baseline.* and /tmp/bench_worker.* removed.
rollback: git checkout HEAD -- /Users/ghost/core/anima/anima-speak/_audio_worker_call.sh; mv /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/stdlib/anima_audio_worker.py /Users/ghost/core/anima/anima-speak/anima_audio_worker.py (restores Phase 2 layout). Note: anima/.gitignore still excludes the .py at the anima-speak path, so this rollback restores the on-disk-only state too. To fully revert, also restore _audio_worker_call.sh's original `WORKER_PY=$(dirname "$0")/anima_audio_worker.py` line.
